note_data.title: Basics of Programming C++
keyPoints: C++ is a high-level programming language developed by Bjarne Stroustrup at Bell Labs in 1983
Program is a set of instructions written in a programming language to solve a specific problem
Algorithm is a step-by-step procedure to solve a particular problem in a finite number of steps
Flowchart is a graphical representation of an algorithm using standard symbols
Pseudocode is an artificial and informal language that helps programmers develop algorithms
C++ program structure includes header files, main() function, and program statements
Variables are memory locations that store data values that can be changed during program execution
Data types in C++ include int, float, char, and double to specify the type of data
Input/output operations use cin for input and cout for output with iostream header file

definitions.definition: A program is a set of instructions written in a programming language to solve a specific problem or to perform a particular task on a computer.
An algorithm is a step-by-step procedure or method for solving a problem in a finite number of steps.
A flowchart is a graphical representation of an algorithm or program using standard symbols to show the flow of control.
A variable is a named memory location that stores data values which can be changed during program execution.
C++ is a high-level, object-oriented programming language developed by Bjarne Stroustrup at Bell Labs in 1983.

importantQuestions.question: Define algorithm and write its characteristics.
importantQuestions.answer: Algorithm is a step-by-step procedure to solve a problem in finite steps. Characteristics: (1) Input (2) Output (3) Definiteness (4) Finiteness (5) Effectiveness

importantQuestions.question: Write the structure of C++ program with example. Also explain different data types used in C++.
importantQuestions.answer: C++ Program Structure: (1) Header files (#include<iostream>) (2) Using namespace std (3) Main function int main() (4) Program statements (5) Return 0. Data types: int (whole numbers), float (decimal numbers), char (single character), double (large decimal numbers). Example program showing variable declaration and basic input/output operations.

mcqs.question: C++ programming language was developed by:
Which header file is used for input/output operations in C++?
The symbol used for output in C++ is:

commonMistakes: Students often forget to write #include<iostream> header file in C++ programs
Not drawing proper flowchart symbols - oval for start/end, rectangle for process, diamond for decision
Forgetting semicolon (;) at the end of C++ statements
Writing algorithm steps in programming language instead of simple English
examTips: This chapter usually has 2-3 MCQs and 1 long question in every BSEK paper
Always draw flowchart with proper symbols and flow direction
Memorize exact definitions - they appear word-for-word in exams
Practice writing simple C++ programs for calculating area, perimeter etc.
note_data.keyDiagram: Flowchart symbols diagram showing: Oval (Start/End), Rectangle (Process), Diamond (Decision), Parallelogram (Input/Output), Circle (Connector), and arrows showing flow direction. Students must memorize and draw these symbols correctly.
